content:
Sakana Trained One AI to Command GPT-5.5, Opus, and Gemini — It Cracked 73.7 Where They Stalled at 69

9 min read
1 hour ago

--

Two days ago a Tokyo lab shipped a model that scored 73.7 on SWE-Bench Pro. Opus 4.8 gets 69.2 on the same test. GPT-5.5 gets 58.6. Gemini 3.1 Pro gets 54.2. Here is the part that broke my brain: the model that scored 73.7 has no weights of its own for that task. It is GPT-5.5, Opus, and Gemini — the exact models it just beat — wearing a trench coat.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

The model is called Sakana Fugu, it launched June 22, and the cleanest way to describe it is the one Sakana itself uses: an LLM trained to call other LLMs. Not a router. Not a prompt-chaining wrapper you build in LangGraph on a Friday. A foundation model whose actual learned skill is deciding which other foundation model should answer your question, checking the answer, and stitching the good parts together — all behind a single OpenAI-compatible endpoint.
I spent the launch day reading the technical report, the two ICLR papers under it, the benchmark table, and every “isn’t this just OpenRouter?” thread I could find. Some of the hype is justified. Some of it is a category error that Sakana is quietly encouraging. Let me show you both, with the numbers and runnable code, so you can decide where you land.
The number that does the work
